target.title: test(agents): use neutral tool schema fixtures
target.body:
## Summary

- replaces private plugin/tool fixture names in tool-schema tests with neutral `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` names
- keeps this PR test-only so follow-up runtime hardening can land separately

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <14 changed test files> --reporter=verbose` passed 6 shards / 292 tests
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check <14 changed test files>` passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <14 changed test files>` passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed
- added-line and touched-file scrub for the private plugin/tool name returned no matches
- AWS Crabbox `run_b8574973ddbb`, lease `cbx_52f67ecfb0c8`, slug `tidal-lobster`, provider `aws`, type `c7a.8xlarge`: `pnpm check:changed` passed; lease cleaned up
- post-gate rebase onto `origin/main` completed cleanly; diff stayed test-only at 14 files (+116/-114), `git diff --check` passed, and private-name scrub stayed clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: test fixtures no longer use a private plugin/tool name.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree focused test harness plus remote Linux AWS Crabbox changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <14 changed test files> --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm check:changed` on AWS Crabbox run `run_b8574973ddbb`.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed the touched test files; changed gate selected `coreTests` and `extensionTests` and exited 0.

Observed result after fix: branch is neutral-fixture-only again, with no runtime hardening commit included.

What was not tested: no runtime behavior change; this PR intentionally only renames test fixtures.
